statement: Livelock observed: the "Candidate" SPEC_GAP is repeatedly picked, the redundancy perspective flags it, the arbiter REJECTS it as already covered (RFC-0027/RFC-0031), and it rolls back — then re-triggers next scan because the source leaf (containing a "Candidate for a future RFC" phrase) is untouched. The arbiter correctly refuses the work but nothing eliminates the trigger, so the loop reject-loops forever instead of reaching idle. Fix: when the arbiter rejects a SPEC_GAP work order as already-covered (or the redundancy perspective rejects with high confidence and the arbiter confirms), neutralize the triggering source leaf — e.g. set content.specGapResolved=true (or rewrite the triggering phrase) so SPEC_GAP detection in scanDebt skips it thereafter. Persist a DECISION leaf recording the arbiter ruling. Add a regression test: a twice-arbiter-rejected SPEC_GAP source no longer yields a SPEC_GAP debt item.
title: Neutralize arbiter-rejected SPEC_GAP sources so they stop re-dispatching
